title: Data Persistence After App Restart
preconditions: User logged in with completed workout history
testData: Account with at least 1 completed workout
steps:
1. Login to app with test account
2. Complete a workout (log sets, finish workout)
3. Verify workout appears in My Activities
4. Force close the app completely
5. Reopen the app
6. Navigate to My Activities
7. Verify the completed workout is still visible
8. Tap on workout to verify all data (sets, reps, weight) intact
expected:
- Workout history preserved after app restart
- All set/rep/weight data intact
- Workout timestamps correct
- Workout totals (volume, duration) accurate
notes: Data stored in local Drift/SQLite DB (db.sqlite). Activity logs stored as JSON documents. Critical P0 test.

title: Data Persistence After Device Reboot
preconditions: User logged in with completed workout
steps:
1. Login and complete a workout
2. Verify workout in My Activities
3. Fully power off the device
4. Power on the device
5. Open the BB.com app
6. Navigate to My Activities
7. Verify workout data intact
8. Check all movement details preserved
expected:
- Workout data survives device reboot
- Local SQLite database persists
- All workout details accurate
- No data corruption
notes: Tests SQLite persistence across device restarts. Database stored in application documents directory.

title: Data Persistence After Logout/Login
preconditions: User logged in with workout history
steps:
1. Login with test account
2. Note workout count in My Activities
3. Logout from the app
4. Verify local data cleared (cannot access My Activities)
5. Login with same account
6. Navigate to My Activities
7. Verify all previous workouts restored
8. Check workout details match pre-logout state
expected:
- Logout clears local activity logs (eraseActivityLogs called)
- Login triggers fresh sync from server
- All workout history restored after login
- Data matches server state
notes: Logout calls ActivityLogsCubit.clearCubit() which wipes local DB. Login fetches fresh data. Tests server as source of truth.

title: Offline Workout Completion and Sync
preconditions: User logged in, network available initially
steps:
1. Start a workout with network connected
2. Log 1-2 sets
3. Enable airplane mode (go offline)
4. Continue logging sets
5. Complete the workout
6. Verify completion confirmation shown
7. Check My Activities shows the workout
8. Disable airplane mode
9. Wait 10 seconds (sync engine interval is 8 sec)
10. Force close and reopen app
11. Verify workout still present with all data
expected:
- Workout completes successfully offline
- Data saved locally with isDirty flag
- Sync engine uploads when online (8-sec timer)
- No data loss after sync
- Workout appears on other devices after sync
notes: ActivityLogSyncEngine runs every 8 seconds. Dirty flag marks records needing sync. Connectivity listener triggers immediate flush when back online.

title: Incremental Sync After Network Restore
preconditions: User with workout history, network available
steps:
1. Complete a workout with network on
2. Verify sync completes (wait 10 sec)
3. Enable airplane mode
4. Complete another workout offline
5. Note the dirty workout in My Activities
6. Disable airplane mode
7. Wait for sync (up to 10 seconds)
8. Force close app
9. Reopen and verify both workouts present
10. Login on another device - verify both workouts synced
expected:
- Incremental sync uses lastUpdated timestamp
- Only changed records synced
- Dirty flag cleared after successful sync
- Data consistent across devices
notes: Uses 'since' parameter in API to fetch only changed records. Tracks lastUpdated in SharedPreferences.

title: View Historical Workout Data by Date
preconditions: Account with workouts on multiple dates
testData: Workouts completed on at least 3 different dates
steps:
1. Navigate to My Activities
2. Scroll through workout history
3. Verify workouts grouped/listed by date
4. Tap on a past workout
5. Verify workout details load correctly
6. Check all movements and sets visible
7. Navigate back and select different date workout
8. Verify that workout's data is accurate
expected:
- Workouts listed chronologically (newest first)
- Date grouping visible
- Each workout shows: title, duration, date
- Tapping opens WorkoutResultsComponent with full details
notes: My Activities screen sorts by startTime/scheduledDate. Uses ActivityLogsCubit to fetch and display logs.

title: Progress Metrics - Total Volume Calculation
preconditions: Completed workout with multiple exercises
testData: Workout with: 3 sets of 10 reps at 100 lbs (should = 3000 lbs volume)
steps:
1. Complete a workout with known values:
   - Exercise 1: 3 sets x 10 reps x 100 lbs
   - Exercise 2: 3 sets x 8 reps x 50 lbs
2. Finish workout
3. View workout results screen
4. Verify total weight lifted displayed
5. Calculate expected: (3x10x100) + (3x8x50) = 3000 + 1200 = 4200 lbs
6. Verify displayed value matches calculation
7. Check in My Activities that same total shown
expected:
- Total weight/volume calculated correctly
- Server calculates totals on completion
- Results screen shows accurate totalWeight
- Volume = sum of (weight × reps) for all sets
notes: totalWeight field in ActivityLogModel. Calculated server-side. Weight stored as kg in DB, converted to lbs for display if using imperial.

title: Progress Metrics - Workout Duration
preconditions: Ability to complete a timed workout
steps:
1. Note the current time
2. Start a workout
3. Complete sets over ~5 minutes
4. Include a pause (1 minute)
5. Resume and complete workout
6. View workout results
7. Verify activeDuration shown
8. Confirm duration excludes paused time
9. Check total duration includes paused time
expected:
- activeDuration = time actively working out
- Paused time tracked separately in pauseEvents
- Duration displayed in HH:MM:SS format
- Accurate to within reasonable margin
notes: activeDuration field tracks active time. PauseEvents array stores pause/resume timestamps. WorkoutResultsComponent displays formatted duration.

title: Movement History Graph - Weight Progression
preconditions: Multiple workouts with same exercise over time
testData: Same exercise performed on 3+ different days with varying weights
steps:
1. Ensure test data: same movement done multiple days
2. Navigate to Move Library
3. Find the exercise with history
4. Tap to view movement details
5. Look for history/progress graph
6. Verify graph shows weight progression over time
7. Check data points match actual logged weights
8. Tap on graph points for tooltips
expected:
- fl_chart line graph displays correctly
- Y-axis shows weight values
- X-axis shows dates (M/d/yy format)
- Data aggregated by day (max weight per day)
- Smooth curves with animation
- Tooltips show value and date on touch
notes: MovementHistoryGraph uses fl_chart. Groups by day, keeps highest weight per day. Two overlaid charts: weight and reps.

title: Movement History Graph - Reps Progression
preconditions: Multiple workouts with same exercise
testData: Same exercise with varying reps over 3+ sessions
steps:
1. Navigate to exercise history
2. View the progress graph
3. Identify reps line (primary color)
4. Verify reps tracked over time
5. Check data matches logged values
6. Compare weight line vs reps line
7. Test scrolling/zooming if available
expected:
- Reps displayed as separate line
- Right Y-axis shows rep count
- Data points accurate to logged values
- Max reps per day shown
- Can distinguish weight vs reps lines
notes: MovementHistoryGraph overlays two LineChartBarData: one for weight (secondary), one for reps (primary).

title: Workout Results - Complete Summary
preconditions: Just completed workout
steps:
1. Complete a workout with multiple exercises
2. Tap 'Finish Workout'
3. Verify WorkoutResultsComponent appears
4. Check displayed metrics:
   - Active Duration
   - Total Calories (if tracked)
   - Total Distance (if applicable)
   - Total Weight Lifted
5. Verify muscle group visualization
6. Check movement breakdown list
7. Verify heart rate data (if available)
8. Check workout notes section
expected:
- All workout summary metrics displayed
- Duration formatted as HH:MM:SS
- Weight shown in correct units (lbs/kg)
- Body utilization diagram accurate
- Per-movement stats visible
- Notes/mood/energy if entered
notes: Route: /workoutResults. WorkoutResultsComponent displays all ActivityLogModel fields. Supports offline viewing via route extra.

title: Sync Conflict - Local Changes Preserved
preconditions: Two devices logged into same account
steps:
1. Login on Device A
2. Start a workout on Device A
3. Log some sets
4. Enable airplane mode on Device A
5. Continue logging on Device A (offline)
6. On Device B, view the same workout (shows partial data)
7. On Device A, complete workout offline
8. Disable airplane mode on Device A
9. Wait for sync
10. Check Device B - verify Device A's data synced
11. Verify no data loss from Device A
expected:
- Local dirty data takes precedence
- Conflict resolved as 'last write wins'
- Device A's offline changes preserved
- Both devices show consistent data after sync
- No duplicate entries
notes: Sync conflict strategy: if prevIsDirty, skip incoming remote data. Local changes are source of truth until synced.

title: Sync Engine - Differential Updates
preconditions: Network connection, developer tools or network monitoring
steps:
1. Start a workout
2. Log a set (triggers sync)
3. Monitor network requests (if possible)
4. Log another set
5. Observe sync request payload
6. Verify only changed fields in PATCH request
7. Complete workout
8. Verify final sync sends completion data
expected:
- Sync uses PATCH not PUT
- Only changed fields sent (differential)
- No-op syncs skipped (empty payload)
- _lastSent snapshot tracks previous state
- Efficient network usage
notes: ActivityLogSyncEngine._diff() calculates delta from _lastSent. Reduces payload size. May need network inspector to verify.

title: Connectivity Change Triggers Sync
preconditions: User with pending unsync'd workout data
steps:
1. Complete workout while offline
2. Verify data marked dirty locally
3. Keep app in foreground
4. Enable network (disable airplane mode)
5. Observe immediate sync behavior
6. Verify data syncs within seconds
7. Check dirty flag cleared after sync
expected:
- Connectivity listener detects network restore
- Immediate flush triggered (not waiting for 8-sec timer)
- Dirty records synced promptly
- Sync completion clears dirty flags
notes: SyncEngine listens to Connectivity().onConnectivityChanged. Calls flushNow() when connectivity restored.

title: App Background Triggers Sync Flush
preconditions: Workout with unsync'd changes
steps:
1. Start workout
2. Log several sets
3. While in active workout, press home button
4. Wait a moment
5. Check if sync flush triggered
6. Return to app
7. Verify data persisted
8. Force close and reopen
9. Verify workout data intact
expected:
- App lifecycle hook triggers flushNow()
- Data synced before full background
- No data loss if app killed in background
- Sync completes or data preserved locally
notes: SyncEngineLifecycle widget listens to AppLifecycleState changes. Triggers sync on pause/inactive.

title: Offline Media Capture and Sync
preconditions: Feature supports workout photos/videos
steps:
1. Start a workout
2. Enable airplane mode
3. Take a photo or video during workout (if supported)
4. Verify media captured locally
5. Complete workout
6. Check media attached to workout
7. Disable airplane mode
8. Wait for background upload
9. Verify media synced to server
10. Check on another device
expected:
- Media saved to PendingMediaUploadsTable
- Status: 'pending' when offline
- Uploads when online
- Max 3 retry attempts on failure
- Status changes: pending -> uploading -> completed
- Media visible in workout history
notes: Uses PendingMediaLocalDatasource. Media stored locally with localFilePath. Background upload via pending_media system.

title: Historical Movement Data - Copy to Current Workout
preconditions: Active workout with exercise having history
testData: Exercise performed previously with logged weights
steps:
1. Start workout containing an exercise with history
2. Navigate to that exercise
3. Access movement history
4. View previous workout's set data
5. Find option to copy/use previous values
6. Select a previous set's values
7. Verify values populated in current workout
8. Complete current workout with copied values
expected:
- Can view history from within active workout
- Previous set values displayed
- Copy action populates current set fields
- Saves time for users
- Copied values tracked normally
notes: MovementActivityLogsHistory supports copying previous data to current workout. Feature for convenience.

title: Set Statistics Display
preconditions: Completed workout with multiple sets
steps:
1. Complete workout with 3-4 sets per exercise
2. View workout results
3. Find set statistics section
4. Verify Total Sets count accurate
5. Verify Total Reps (or Duration for timed)
6. Check Best Set calculation
7. Compare displayed values to logged data
expected:
- Total Sets = count of all sets
- Total Reps = sum of all reps
- Best Set = max(weight × reps) or max duration
- Statistics accurate to logged data
notes: SetStatistics widget calculates and displays summary. Used in workout summary and results screens.

title: Data Integrity - Corrupted Data Handling
preconditions: Ability to simulate corrupted data (dev tools)
steps:
1. Note: This test may require developer access
2. Attempt to corrupt local database entry
3. Open app
4. Navigate to My Activities
5. Observe app behavior
6. Check for crash vs graceful handling
7. Verify other data unaffected
8. Test recovery: force re-sync from server
expected:
- App handles corrupted data gracefully
- No crash on malformed JSON
- Error logged but app continues
- Other workouts still accessible
- Re-sync can recover data from server
notes: Lower priority edge case. Difficult to test without database access. JSON parsing errors should be caught.

title: Sync Retry on Failure
preconditions: Ability to simulate network failure
steps:
1. Start workout
2. Log some sets (data marked dirty)
3. Simulate network failure during sync
4. Verify data still dirty locally
5. Restore network
6. Wait for next sync cycle (8 seconds)
7. Verify retry succeeds
8. Check dirty flag cleared
expected:
- Sync failure does not lose data
- Dirty flag remains until success
- Next sync cycle retries
- Eventually succeeds when network stable
- No user action required for retry
notes: Fire-and-forget approach. Failed syncs retry automatically on next 8-sec timer tick or connectivity restore.

title: Multiple Users - Data Isolation
preconditions: Two test accounts
testData: Account A with workouts, Account B empty or different
steps:
1. Login as User A
2. Note User A's workout count
3. Logout
4. Login as User B
5. Verify User B's data (different from A)
6. No User A workouts visible
7. Complete workout as User B
8. Logout
9. Login as User A
10. Verify only User A's workouts (plus none of B's)
expected:
- Complete data isolation between users
- Logout clears local cache
- Login fetches only that user's data
- No data leakage between accounts
notes: eraseActivityLogs() called on logout. Fresh sync on login. Critical for multi-user device scenarios.

title: Resume Paused Workout - Data Preserved
preconditions: Paused workout exists
steps:
1. Start a workout
2. Log 2-3 sets with specific values
3. Exit workout (pause)
4. Force close app
5. Reopen app
6. Find paused workout in My Activities
7. Resume the workout
8. Verify all previously logged sets intact
9. Verify set values match exactly
10. Complete the workout
expected:
- Paused workout visible in My Activities
- Resume loads all previous data
- Set values exactly as logged before
- Can continue from where left off
- No data loss on pause/resume cycle
notes: Uses pausedActivityLogId to resume. Data stored locally with status='paused'. Critical user flow.

title: Workout Notes and Mood Persistence
preconditions: Workout with notes feature
steps:
1. Start and complete a workout
2. During or after, add workout notes
3. Set mood/energy level (if available)
4. Finish and save workout
5. View workout in history
6. Verify notes visible
7. Check mood/energy saved
8. Force close, reopen, verify persistence
expected:
- Text notes saved with workout
- Mood (int) persisted
- Energy level persisted
- Level of effort persisted
- All metadata survives app restart
notes: ActivityLogModel fields: mood, energyLevel, levelOfEffort, notes. Part of workout journal feature.

title: Health Data Sync - Heart Rate, Steps
preconditions: iOS device with HealthKit enabled, workout with health tracking
testData: Workout while wearing Apple Watch or heart rate monitor
steps:
1. Grant HealthKit permissions to app
2. Wear heart rate monitor or Apple Watch
3. Start and complete workout
4. Check workout results
5. Verify heart rate data captured
6. Check heart rate chart displayed
7. Verify steps counted during workout
8. Check distance if applicable
expected:
- healthLogs in ActivityLogModel populated
- Heart rate array with timestamps
- Steps count tracked
- Distance measured
- Data persists with workout
- Chart visualizes heart rate over time
notes: HealthLogs model contains: heartRate, distance, steps arrays. HealthCubit manages permissions and data fetch.
